title: nassella coquimbensis
intro: Nassella coquimbensis, also known as Coquimbo fescue, is a type of grass native to South America, particularly in Chile. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Nassella coquimbensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Nassella coquimbensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine in the form of infusions, decoctions, or powdered extracts.
